input: Run a full 8-phase safety assessment on aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid). This is a well-characterized compound expected to have a clean drug-likeness profile.
Run a full safety assessment on thalidomide. This compound has a notorious teratogenicity history.

expected_details.teratogenicity: CRITICAL — category X; absolute contraindication in pregnancy
expected_details.mechanism_of_teratogenicity: cereblon binding → degradation of SALL4 and other transcription factors critical for limb development
expected_details.current_use: approved for multiple myeloma, ENL (with strict REMS — Thalomid REMS)

input: Run a safety assessment on curcumin (diferuloylmethane). This is a well-known PAINS compound.

expected_details.drug_likeness: poor — PAINS alerts make bioassay data unreliable
expected_details.clinical_reality: no approved drug despite thousands of publications; bioavailability extremely low

input: Run a safety assessment on terfenadine (Seldane). This was withdrawn due to cardiac toxicity.

expected_details.cardiac_toxicity: QT prolongation → torsades de pointes → sudden cardiac death
expected_details.withdrawal_reason: hERG-mediated cardiac arrhythmia, especially with CYP3A4 inhibitors
expected_details.cyp3a4_interaction: terfenadine is a CYP3A4 substrate; ketoconazole/erythromycin co-administration caused fatal arrhythmias

input: Run a safety assessment on cyclosporine. This is a known Lipinski Rule of 5 violator.
Assess the safety of nitrosamine impurity NDMA (N-nitrosodimethylamine) found as a contaminant in pharmaceutical products. Apply ICH M7 principles.

expected_details.genotoxicity: positive (AMES test positive; known DNA alkylating agent)
expected_details.context: ranitidine, valsartan, metformin recalls due to NDMA contamination

input: Assess a hypothetical compound with: MW 350, LogP 2.1, HBD 1, HBA 4, TPSA 60, 3 rotatable bonds, no PAINS alerts, no structural alerts, hERG IC50 >30 μM, no CYP inhibition. Classify the safety profile.

expected_details.recommendation: proceed to standard safety testing program

input: Assess hepatotoxicity risk for a compound with: daily dose 500 mg, lipophilic (LogP 4.5), forms reactive metabolites via CYP3A4 (glutathione conjugate detected), and inhibits BSEP (bile salt export pump) at 5 μM.

dili_risk_factors: high daily dose (>100 mg — Lammert rule)
expected_details.bsep_concern: BSEP IC50 5 μM is concerning — cholestatic liver injury risk
expected_details.reactive_metabolite: CYP3A4 bioactivation → reactive intermediate → GSH depletion → hepatotoxicity
expected_details.daily_dose_rule: daily dose >100 mg + lipophilic + reactive metabolite = high DILI risk (rule of 2)
expected_details.recommendation: conduct dedicated hepatotoxicity studies; consider dose reduction or structural modification

input: A compound tests AMES-positive in TA98 (frameshift mutation). However, it is intended as an oncology drug with no alternative. How should genotoxicity data be interpreted?

expected_details.interpretation: AMES positive does not automatically preclude development for life-threatening indications
expected_details.ich_s9_guidance: ICH S9 allows reduced genotoxicity package for oncology drugs with serious conditions
expected_details.risk_benefit: acceptable if: disease is life-threatening, no alternatives exist, and benefit outweighs genotoxic risk

input: Assess a novel compound that is structurally very similar (Tanimoto 0.92) to a known hepatotoxin that was withdrawn from market. No direct toxicology data available for the new compound. Apply read-across principles.

expected_details.tanimoto_threshold: 0.92 is high structural similarity — read-across is justified
expected_details.inference: structural analog was hepatotoxic → new compound has high probability of similar liability
limitations: small structural changes can alter toxicity
